evals.name: api syntax docs ctx7 cli
evals.prompt: A user asks: how do I use FastAPI dependency overrides in tests? I need syntax and examples. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses the context7-cli documentation workflow, resolves the library with ctx7 library, queries docs with ctx7 docs, and avoids broad comparison-style web research.
assertions: The output treats the request as API documentation lookup rather than broad web research.
The output mentions identifying the library/framework version from project files when possible.
The output uses ctx7 library to resolve the library unless a /org/project ID is already provided.
The output uses ctx7 docs to fetch syntax and examples for the selected library ID.
The output says to use npx ctx7@latest if ctx7 is missing.
The output says to use broader web research only if docs lookup is insufficient or the task changes to comparison/recent facts.

evals.name: boundary comparison request
evals.prompt: A user asks: compare FastAPI vs Django Ninja for a new backend. Describe how this docs skill should respond.
evals.expected_output: The response identifies this as a comparison/research task and routes to researching-web instead of only looking up API docs.
assertions: The output says the request is a comparison rather than a narrow API docs lookup.
The output routes to researching-web or a research workflow.
The output avoids pretending docs lookup alone is enough for trade-off comparison.
The output may offer to use docs lookup later for chosen-framework syntax with ctx7 library and ctx7 docs.
